title: Add buffer option to spice backend
body:
I'm experimenting with doing mixed signal simulations with NgSpice and Yosys, similar to https://www.isotel.eu/mixedsim/intro/concept.html

Instead of a custom synth script, I'm basically trying to use the output of `synth` directly. Either way you end up having to define all the subcircuit models.

Rather than CMOS transistors, my models use the XSPICE event-driven digital models, which should be much faster than a full analog CMOS simulation. However, Yosys currently inserts DC sources to connect nets together, which is incompatible with digital signals.

So this PR introduces a `-buf` option that allows you to specify a subcircuit implementation instead of the DC source. From there you can just use the digital `d_buffer` cell to implement it. The default still uses the DC source, which works fine for analog simulations.

If there is any interest I could prepare a PR to upstream the XSPICE cell library, and maybe a `synth_xspice` command. My library basically implements most of the internal fine cells produced by `synth`, but needs an extra `dfflegalize` to handle all the flops that are not supported. Library: https://gist.github.com/pepijndevos/2f41507b821b9a3db65beb68d3330b3b Synth commands:
```
read_verilog -lib -icells +/simcells.v
synth -flatten
dfflegalize -cell \$_DFFSR_PPP_ 0
stat
write_spice -buf __BUF_ blinky.cir
```

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/168609/104481868-8f3da700-55c6-11eb-897b-662dba5bb257.png)
